query: Inspect the next available harness maintenance issue and start work only if claimable work exists. If no claimable issue exists, report that state and do not modify files.
fixture_intent: Normal scenario: inspect-and-claim discipline — bd prime, bd ready, atomic claim or explicit no-work report.
expected_skill_contribution: The Beads skill should inspect durable ready state, claim work only when claimable work exists, and otherwise report the no-work state without modifying files.
baseline_gaps: Agent may choose work without checking durable ready state.
Agent may not claim work atomically before starting.
expected_behavior: Runs `bd prime` and `bd ready --json` before any other action.
Claims selected work with `bd update <id> --claim --json` before any modification when claimable work exists.
When no claimable work exists, explicitly reports that state and makes no file edits or Beads mutations.
Summarises selected scope and blockers, or summarises the no-work-available state, then stops.

query: A task to validate recipe YAML must wait for the task that edits `.goose/recipes/harness-master.yaml`; encode this dependency in Beads.
fixture_intent: Very difficult scenario: dependency direction, requirement language, verification — `bd dep add B A` means B needs A.
expected_skill_contribution: The Beads skill should encode the dependency using `bd dep add <B> <A>` (B needs A), explicitly reject 'A blocks B' framing, create both beads if they don't exist, verify with `bd blocked --json` and report the actual blocked output showing the validate-bead is not claimable — a baseline agent either reverses the direction or skips verification output.
baseline_gaps: Agent may reverse the dependency direction (add edit-bead as dependent on validate-bead).
Agent states 'dependency added' without running a verification query or showing its output.
Agent uses 'blocks' language ('edit blocks validate') rather than requirement language ('validate needs edit').
expected_behavior: Creates both beads (edit-harness-master and validate-recipe) if they do not already exist before adding the dependency.
Uses `bd dep add <validate-id> <edit-id>` — the validate-bead is the child (it needs the edit-bead), not the reverse.
Explicitly rejects 'blocks' framing: states the dependency as 'validate needs edit' or 'validate depends on edit', never 'edit blocks validate'.
Runs `bd blocked --json` or `bd ready --json` after adding the dependency and reports the actual command output showing the validate-bead is blocked/not-ready.

query: Store a durable pointer memory that the canonical release checklist for this harness is in docs/10-release-readiness.md; avoid copying the checklist content or creating task files.
fixture_intent: Difficult scenario: pointer memory discipline — 'read when' trigger phrase required (not 'read before'), short key, no content dump, no MEMORY.md. Skill gotcha now explicitly names this distinction.
expected_skill_contribution: The Beads skill should store a short pointer memory with a stable key using the exact 'read when <trigger>' wording (not 'read before'), avoiding long content, MEMORY.md, or markdown TODO tracking. The skill now includes a verbatim template and a gotcha for the 'read when' vs 'read before' distinction.
baseline_gaps: Agent may store long-form checklist content in memory instead of a pointer.
Agent may create MEMORY.md or markdown TODO files.
expected_behavior: Stores or proposes a `bd remember` command with a stable kebab-case `--key`.

Uses the pointer format with `read when <trigger>` (not 'read before') — e.g. 'Release checklist: canonical source is docs/10-release-readiness.md; read when preparing a release; invariant: <rule>' — under 250 characters. The `#section` anchor is optional when the file has no named sections.
Does not copy the checklist content into the memory value.
Does not create MEMORY.md, markdown TODO files, or Beads task issues for this memory.
